text.title: Pipeline Types and Modern Data Stack
text.summary: Batch vs streaming vs Lambda/Kappa trade-offs, ETL vs ELT decision, medallion architecture (Bronze/Silver/Gold), and the modern data stack component map.

rule.statement: Choose streaming only when the use case genuinely requires sub-second latency; default to batch for all other analytical workloads.
rule.rationale: Streaming is significantly more complex to operate (exactly-once semantics, schema evolution, consumer lag monitoring). Batch pipelines are easier to debug, backfill, and test. Reserve streaming for fraud detection, real-time dashboards, and monitoring pipelines.

list.item: Batch: minutes-to-hours latency, Spark/Airflow/dbt. Use for ML training, reporting, historical analysis.
Streaming: milliseconds-to-seconds, Kafka/Flink/Spark Streaming. Use for fraud detection, real-time analytics, CDC replication.
Lambda: batch layer for accuracy + speed layer for freshness. Higher complexity; consider Kappa first.
Kappa: streaming-only with event replay. Simpler than Lambda; use when all historical reprocessing can be done via stream replay.

rule.statement: Prefer ELT over ETL for cloud warehouse environments (Snowflake, BigQuery, Databricks) where warehouse compute is elastic.
rule.rationale: ELT preserves raw source data in the bronze layer, enabling re-transformation when business rules change without re-ingesting from source. ETL discards raw data after transformation.

list.item: Use ETL when: strict compliance requires data anonymisation before storage (HIPAA/GDPR), legacy target systems have limited compute, small data volume with complex transforms.
Use ELT when: cloud-native warehouse available, large volumes needing distributed compute, need raw data preservation for future use cases.

rule.statement: Implement Bronze/Silver/Gold medallion layers where each layer has an explicit owner, quality contract, and materialisation strategy.
rule.rationale: Without explicit layer contracts, data quality issues propagate silently from ingestion to analytics. 68% of cloud-first enterprises use this pattern (2025) precisely because it enforces quality gates at each transformation boundary.

list.item: Bronze: raw ingestion, unprocessed as-is from source, append-only, never delete
Silver: standardised schema, deduplicated, type-enforced, no business logic
Gold: aggregated, enriched, business-ready, optimised for query patterns

section.title: Modern Data Stack
section.p: Component map by function: Ingestion (Airbyte, Fivetran, Debezium), Storage (S3/GCS/ADLS), Warehouse (Snowflake/BigQuery/Databricks), Transformation (dbt/Spark), Orchestration (Airflow/Dagster/Prefect), Quality (Great Expectations/Soda/dbt tests), Catalog (Atlan/Alation).
